fields.label: Do you have 2–5 years in product management, product ops, BizOps, strategy, technical program management, revenue operations, or a similar area?

Please submit a portfolio snippet of 2–3 AI artifacts (e.g., Loom demo, written outline, mini video, flow diagram) that show a shipped AI-powered workflow or AI product you’ve built and its tangible impact.
fields.value: Throughout my career at Microsoft and in earlier customer-facing engineering roles, I consistently prioritised user impact as the primary success metric. I build strong empathy by embedding with customer teams, running discovery interviews, and tracking adoption patterns to validate that shipped features actually solve the stated problem.
fields.source: cover_essay_short ('Please submit a portfolio snippet of 2–3')

fields.label: The role requires deep hands-on experience building with AI. Describe a project where you leveraged core LLM concepts (e.g., prompting, fine-tuning, embeddings, retrieval) and translate them into a reliable, user-facing product. What was the greatest technical challenge you faced, and how did you resolve it?
fields.value: Yes. I have hands-on experience in this area through my work at Microsoft on the Resilience Automation Platform and earlier roles in customer-facing engineering.

fields.label: Walk us through a time you "vibe-coded a prototype before lunch and wrote the rollout plan after." Describe a messy operational problem you turned into an elegant automated system using AI, detailing the full journey from prototype to scaled production.
